questions.text: What is your gender? (Select all that apply)
Who are you interested in meeting? (Select all that apply)
What are you looking for? (Select all that apply)

options.label: Not sure yet / Open to anything

questions.text: How do you prefer to communicate when you're interested in someone?

options.label: Text or message first, then progress to calls
Jump straight to phone or video calls
Meet in person as soon as possible
Take it slow with occasional messages

questions.text: In a conflict with a partner, you're most likely to:

options.label: Address it immediately and talk it through
Take time to cool off, then discuss calmly
Try to smooth things over with humor or affection

questions.text: How often do you expect to hear from someone you're dating?

options.label: At least once daily with good conversations
Every few days is perfectly fine
When there's something meaningful to share

questions.text: How important is it that your partner appreciates intellectual or cultural discussions?

options.label: Very important - we should engage in deep conversations regularly
Somewhat important - occasional meaningful discussions are nice
Not very important - we can have different intellectual interests
Not important - I prefer light, casual conversation

questions.text: When someone shares something vulnerable with you, you:

options.label: Open up equally to create mutual vulnerability
Listen deeply and ask thoughtful questions
Respect it but maintain some emotional boundaries

questions.text: How do you express affection?

options.label: Words of affirmation and verbal expressions
Acts of service and thoughtful gestures

questions.text: Which is worse?

options.label: People who use 'your' when they mean 'you're'

questions.text: How comfortable are you saying 'I love you' first?

options.label: Very – I say it when I feel it
Somewhat – if the moment feels right
Prefer to wait for them to say it first
Very cautious – need to be absolutely sure

questions.text: Your texting style in a relationship is best described as:
How do you feel about double texting?

options.label: Totally fine, I do it all the time
Okay occasionally if I have something to add
Never. I wait for them to respond.

questions.text: If you see someone attractive reading a book in public, would you approach them?

options.label: Maybe if we made eye contact first
Probably not, I'd feel like I'm interrupting
Never, that would be way too awkward

questions.text: Should burning the American flag be illegal?

options.label: I don't have strong feelings about this

questions.text: How do you feel about voice memos?

options.label: Love them! So much better than typing
Prefer text but will listen if someone sends one

questions.text: Someone uses 'literally' when they mean 'figuratively.' You:
What does 'good communication' mean to you?

options.label: Being able to discuss anything openly and honestly
Understanding each other without always needing words
Respecting each other's need for privacy

questions.text: Your date's phone buzzes constantly during dinner. They don't check it. You:

options.label: Appreciate their focus on our date
Suggest they check in case it's important
Get slightly annoyed by the buzzing

questions.text: How important is intellectual compatibility?
You're about to sleep with someone for the first time. Do you leave the lights on?
How long should the ideal hug last?

options.label: As long as it feels right

questions.text: Would you go through your partner's phone if they left it unlocked?

options.label: Never, that's a violation of privacy
Only if I had a reason to be suspicious

questions.text: How important is physical chemistry on a first date?

options.label: Crucial – I need to feel that spark immediately
Important, but can develop over time
Somewhat important, but personality matters more
Not very – I need emotional connection first

questions.text: When do you typically feel ready for physical intimacy?

options.label: After a few great dates and conversations

questions.text: How do you feel about sleeping over on the first date?

options.label: If the vibe is right, why not?
Open to it but no expectations

questions.text: How do you feel about public displays of affection?

options.label: Enjoy it in moderation (hand-holding, light kisses)
Prefer to keep it minimal in public
Uncomfortable with PDA, prefer private affection

questions.text: Do you prefer to be the big spoon or little spoon?

options.label: I need my space when I sleep

questions.text: What makes you feel most connected to a partner?

options.label: Comfortable silence and just being together

questions.text: What's your approach to celebrating relationship milestones?

options.label: Big celebrations – I love marking special occasions
Don't really track or celebrate milestones

questions.text: How do you feel about morning breath kisses?

options.label: Totally fine, comes with the territory

questions.text: Your ideal level of couple time vs. alone time is:

options.label: Together most of the time – I love being with my partner
Significant couple time with some solo activities
Lots of independence with regular quality time

questions.text: Would you date someone who was in a committed non-monogamous relationship?

options.label: Probably not, but I'm not judging

questions.text: Should your partner be your best friend?

options.label: No, I keep friendship and romance separate

questions.text: How would you feel if your partner wanted to shower together regularly?

options.label: I need my shower time alone

questions.text: Your partner wants to go to sleep but you want to keep talking. You:

Let them sleep, we'll talk tomorrow

questions.text: How important is it that you and your partner have similar sex drives?

options.label: Very important but we can compromise

questions.text: Would you rather:

options.label: Have amazing sex with someone you barely know

questions.text: You're getting intimate and your partner says 'I love you' for the first time. You don't feel the same yet. You:

options.label: Say it back to make them feel good

options.label: Completely joint – what's mine is ours

options.label: Open to it but would rather wait

archetypes.name: The Deep Connector
archetypes.description: You approach connections with intention and care. You value meaningful conversations, open communication, and building something real over time.
archetypes.ideal_match: Someone who appreciates depth and genuine connection
archetypes.growth_tip: Don't overthink – sometimes it's okay to just enjoy the moment

archetypes.name: The Enthusiast
archetypes.description: You dive into connections wholeheartedly and aren't afraid to show your excitement. You're affectionate, expressive, and thrive when building meaningful bonds with others.
archetypes.ideal_match: Someone who matches your enthusiasm and appreciates your energy

archetypes.name: The Independent Spirit
archetypes.description: You value your autonomy and bring a strong sense of self to your connections. You're looking for people who enhance your life without defining it.
archetypes.ideal_match: Someone confident who has their own full life
archetypes.growth_tip: Remember to make time for vulnerability and closeness

archetypes.name: The Explorer
archetypes.description: You want people to experience life with. You value spontaneity, new adventures, and connections that feel exciting and dynamic.
archetypes.ideal_match: Someone who's up for anything and shares your zest for life
archetypes.growth_tip: Don't forget to create stability amidst the excitement

archetypes.name: The Steady Builder
archetypes.description: You believe the best connections develop gradually. You need time to build trust and prefer letting a solid foundation form before deepening any bond.
archetypes.ideal_match: Someone patient who understands that good things take time
archetypes.growth_tip: Be open to unexpected connections that don't fit your timeline

archetypes.name: The Pragmatist
archetypes.description: You approach connections with your head and your heart. You value compatibility, shared goals, and building something together that makes sense.
archetypes.ideal_match: Someone who shares your vision and values
archetypes.growth_tip: Leave room for spontaneity and surprises

archetypes.name: The Free Spirit
archetypes.description: You're open to wherever life takes you. You don't believe in forcing connections and prefer to let things unfold naturally without pressure.
archetypes.ideal_match: Someone who doesn't need labels or timelines
archetypes.growth_tip: Communicate your needs even when going with the flow

archetypes.name: The Well-Rounded
archetypes.description: You've found equilibrium between independence and togetherness, excitement and stability, planning and spontaneity. You bring balanced energy to your connections.
archetypes.ideal_match: Someone who appreciates your balanced approach
archetypes.growth_tip: Don't be afraid to lean into your preferences more strongly
